steps.title: Find the sample proportion
steps.content: In the sample, $6$ of $24$ lawns contain fertilizer: $$\frac{6}{24}=0.25$$

steps.title: Scale to the town
steps.content: Apply the same proportion to $288$ lawns: $$0.25\cdot288=72$$

steps.title: Check the estimate against the choices
steps.content: Since $6$ out of $24$ is one-fourth of the sample, the estimate should be one-fourth of $288$. One-fourth of $288$ is $72$, not a very small number like $5$, $4$, or $12$.
